TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr-story_pani

S.FORM: te ku poisua niaranai kita Kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 我要講關於我們Kanakanavu是從哪裡來的。
I am talking about where we Kanakanavu are from.

S.FORM: cʉroo tanairʉ sua tararakaua taniarʉ ia isua to’onaa racʉnga misee.
S.TRANSL: 你看著太陽，太陽出現的地方，那裡就是Racʉnga的地方。
Look, where the sun arises is the place where Racʉnga was.

S.FORM: ’esi ku pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ hlcʉna mataa nacʉnga matʉa kara ’ʉnai isi.
S.TRANSL: 我在思索著hlcʉna跟nacʉnga這兩個地名是同一個的地方嗎？
I am thinking if the two locations mean the same place.

S.FORM: sua isi ia pacʉpʉngʉ cu maku masini.
S.TRANSL: 這些到現在我還在思索。
Until now, I am still thinking about this question.

S.FORM: rancurunga ia ’esi na masini to’onaa mita.
S.TRANSL: Rancurunga這個地方在現在我們所住的地方。
Rancurunga is located at the place where we lived now.

S.FORM: ʉncoo uh… uh… ’esi na masini Namasia.
S.TRANSL: 現在那瑪夏區的…
It is now at…

S.FORM: ʉncoo amuseni kʉcʉ ’ʉnai isua?.
S.TRANSL: 那個地名究竟怎麼稱呼去了？
What is the name of that place?

S.FORM: uh… ah! ’esi na nangisarʉ tamna cimʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 那個位置在屬於南沙魯里的山上。
It is located at the mountain of Nangisarʉ.

S.FORM: ’una ’ʉnai taca cacaru in ia tʉnʉkai in ia ’una ’ʉnai rancurunga kisʉʉn mita Kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 在他的頂端有一個地方卡那卡那富稱其為Rangurunga。
At the crown layer of the trees, there is a place we call Rangurunga.

S.FORM: nimoon ’inia sasarunai Kanakanvu ’uma’anupu aracakanʉ ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 一個卡那卡那富男子帶著他的狗打獵。
There was a Kanakanavu male hunting with his dog.

S.FORM: sua nericeni tacau ia ’una kan nipivaru.
S.TRANSL: 據說他所帶的狗有懷孕的。
There were dogs who were having babies.

S.FORM: ’una nikamanumanu tacau.
S.TRANSL: 有生出小狗的母狗。
There were dogs who have given birth.

S.FORM: mia turupang cu nguani aracakanʉ ’inia ia nevici nguani tacau isia isua pue’i na Racʉnga.
S.TRANSL: 他們在那裡結束打獵之後，他們帶著那些狗回到Racʉnga.
When they finished their hunting there, they brought those dogs back to Racʉnga.

S.FORM: nakai sua nikamanumanu tacau isua ia takacicin mavici tamtitu pue’i na rancurunga.
S.TRANSL: 但是那隻生小孩的母狗獨自帶牠的小狗回到Rancurunga。
However, the dog mother bought her doggies back to Rancurunga herself.

S.FORM: makasua kani popue’i manasʉ ’una makauturu.
S.TRANSL: 據說那樣來來回回有三次
It is said that the dog came and went like that for three times.

S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ cu mamarang Kanakanavu ’una kara vanai neni popue’i sua ah tacau umavici tam tamtitu in puei’i na rancurunga.
S.TRANSL: 當時卡族的老人家就想著狗狗那樣來來回回究竟有什麼原因。
The Kankanavu elders thought why the dog kept bringing her doggies to Rancurung and back again.

S.FORM: makasua cu kan eh ʉncoo mamarang Kanakanavu manasʉ ’e tumatutuuru tikana te kita aravari ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 因此，當時卡那卡那富老人家就想著或許狗狗是在告訴他們，他們該搬家了。
Therefore, elders at that time thought perhaps (those dogs) were telling them that they should move.

S.FORM: nakai pacʉpʉcʉpʉng ’akia nannakʉ uh ’inia ah ka’an mamanʉng siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 但是又想沒有女人在那裡生活很不方便。
However, they thought without females, they could not have good lives there.

S.FORM: imo pui’i na racurunga umavici nanakʉ in mamanu mon cu ’inia cumacʉvʉrʉ mumʉkʉ ka’um’uma ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 所以，他們回到nacʉnga要帶他們的女人和小孩到Racurunga那裏生活、墾地、種田。
SO, they returned to nacʉnga to bring their women and children to Rancurunga to live and farm there.

S.FORM: makasua cu kangvang.
S.TRANSL: 也都那樣。
They also did that.

S.FORM: moca kangvang 'inia.
S.TRANSL: 也都搬去那裡。
They also moved to that place.

S.FORM: mon cu na Rancurunga ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 就在Rancurunga那個地方。
They lived in Rangurunga.

S.FORM: ka’umo’uma mon ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 那裡開墾田園。
They farmed that place.

S.FORM: katatanasa.
S.TRANSL: 蓋房子。
They built houses.

S.FORM: mon ’inia macasU taniara.
S.TRANSL: 他們在那邊待得很久。
They had lived there for a long time.

S.FORM: nakai mia aratatia cu ku manasʉ masʉpatʉnʉ cu cenana ’una kanakanavu cau kanakanavu makasi miana kani kanakanavu ia nimon na tainan Anping kisʉʉn masini niarakukun kan ʉncoo.
S.TRANSL: 但是當我長大之後，大約到了四十歲，有卡族人說以前卡族人曾經住在台南，現在叫做安平的地方，和平埔族一起生活。
However, when I grew up, at about forty years old, I heard some Kanakanavu said once Kanakanavu lived with Pingpu tribes at An-Ping, Tainan.

S.FORM: mia ’esi na meinon mararuai kani cau.
S.TRANSL: 當在美濃的時候，人們分道揚鑣。
They went separate ways when they were in Meinong.

S.FORM: sua ’una kani cau mutʉkʉtʉkʉ mokusa Uncoo meinon mokusa na riukue mokusa na masini tauien.
S.TRANSL: 據說有的人直走去美濃，去六龜，去桃源。
It is said that some people walked to Meinong, Liugue, and Tauyuen directly.

S.FORM: ’una cu kari makasi sua nguani kani ia nguai sua masini ra’arua misee.
S.TRANSL: 就有這麼一說，他們就是現在的拉阿魯哇族。
There is a word saying that they are Ra’arua that we know now.

S.FORM: ’una kani cau cucumeni mon na cisan mararuai maica na namasia mokusa na masini to’ona mita.
S.TRANSL: 據說還有其他的人在旗山分道走了，經過那瑪夏，到達現在我們的地方。
It is also said that some other people chose another way to go, passing Namasia to where we are living now.

S.FORM: sua isua kani ia nguai sua ikita kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 那些也就是我們卡那卡那富族人。
Those people are we Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: makasua tinimana maku.
S.TRANSL: 我所聽到的是那樣。
What I’ve heard is like that.

S.FORM: ’una kani cu niatuumura canumu.
S.TRANSL: 據說有滿出來的水。
It is also said there was overflowing water.

S.FORM: ’una kani vʉrʉnga.
S.TRANSL: 據說有鰻魚。
And, an eel.

S.FORM: nimon kan na canumu isua tʉpʉn kee canumu.
S.TRANSL: 據說他在水中堵住了水流。
It is said that it stuck the water.

S.FORM: ara’itumuru canumu.
S.TRANSL: 水變多了。
The water became much more.

S.FORM: nipunarU kan kangvang Unco kanakanavu tamna ’umo’uma tanasa.
S.TRANSL: 卡族的耕地以及家都被沖走。
All the fields and houses of Kanakanavu are washed away.

S.FORM: imua manguru kanakanavu mokusa tʉnʉkai ʉnco cimʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 所以卡族人逃到山頂去。
Therefore, Kanakanavu escaped their homes to the top of mountains.

S.FORM: mesua kan ia ’akia kan apuru.
S.TRANSL: 據說那個時候沒有火。
At that time, there was no fire.

S.FORM: nakai cumacʉ’ʉra cimʉrʉ cucumen na Tanʉ’ʉcʉ kan.
S.TRANSL: 據說看到在Tanʉ’ʉcʉ的山。
But, they saw another mountain in Tanʉ’ʉcʉ.

S.FORM: ’una kan apuru.
S.TRANSL: 據說有火。
It is said that there had fire.

S.FORM: ’una kan to’urung.
S.TRANSL: 有一隻山羌。
There was a muntjac.

S.FORM: makasi sua tia iku mokusa ’inia mokusa tana’ʉcʉ 'inia umara ’apuru misee.
S.TRANSL: 山羌說：「我要去tana’ʉcʉ拿回火種。」
The muntjac said, ‘I will go to tana’ʉcʉ to get the fire.’

S.FORM: marivivini ’una kan tapinange urune kisUUn tapinange isua.
S.TRANSL: 最後，有隻鳥，被稱作urune
At last, there was a bird, Urune.

S.FORM: tapinange isua ia miana kani nguani nguani tamna ivici ia ka’an masinang.
S.TRANSL: 那種鳥，據說以前他們的嘴巴不是紅色的。
it is said that the pecks of those birds were not red.

S.FORM: pasin kan kee cau mesua tia nguain kan mokusa na a sipara isa umara ’apuru.
S.TRANSL: 鳥對人說由他來去那個對岸拿火種。
The bird said to human beings that he will go to the other side of the river to get the fire back’

S.FORM: imo aranai meesua ’una cani mita poisua urune isua.
S.TRANSL: 所以從那時候起，我們有一首關於紅嘴黑鵯的歌。
So from then on, we have a song singing about that Himalayan.Black.Bulbul.

S.FORM: makasua cu mon na mon Kanakanavu na Namasia isi.
S.TRANSL: 那般之後，卡那卡那富族人就住在那瑪夏這裡。
Therefore, Kanakanavu resided there.

S.FORM: nakai aranai meesua koo cu arivari marivari arivari na cucumeni to’ona kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 但是從那時候，我們就沒有再遷移到別的地方了。
Since then, we did not move to any other places.

S.FORM: sua kanakanavu mon cu na Namasia isi.
S.TRANSL: 卡那卡那富族人就一直待在那瑪夏這個地方。
Kanakanavu stay at Namasia.

S.FORM: nakai miana ’una cau cucumen noo makari kitana kanakanavu ia makasi sua ikita kan ia kisaa Sumukun taivuran misee.
S.TRANSL: 但是，以前有其他人在跟我們卡族人談話時, 叫我們卡族Taivuran，布農族也那樣稱呼我們。
However, in the past, there are some other people called us, as what Bunun called, Taivuran, when they were talking to us.

S.FORM: makasua cu nimon manasu nimon cu kita na namasia isi upeen kusai?.
S.TRANSL: 那樣，我們卡那卡那富族待在那瑪夏這裡究竟有多久了？
Then, how long have we Kanakanavu lived here?

S.FORM: manasʉ ’una usʉpatʉ nʉmʉ nokai ’una urima nʉmʉ nimon kita na namasia isi cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我們待在那瑪夏這裡生長大概有四百或五百年了。
Perhaps We have lived in Namasia and grew up there for four hundred or five hundred years.

S.FORM: makasia sua sua tia tuturua maku sosomanpe.
S.TRANSL: 我所要講的就是這樣。謝謝。
This is all I want to tell you. Thank you.
